title: To remove the lesser prairie-chicken from the lists of threatened species and endangered species published pursuant to the Endangered Species Act of 1973 and to amend that Act to exclude the lesser prairie-chicken from the authority of that Act.
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.
plain_summary: This bill removes the lesser prairie-chicken from the government's list of protected animals that are in danger of disappearing forever. The bird is currently protected because there are not many of them left in the wild. The bill also changes the law so that the government can never put the lesser prairie-chicken back on the protected list in the future. This means the bird would not get special protection even if its numbers got very low again.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1  Delisting of Lesser Prairie-Chicken
sections.text: Part A says that no matter what any other law says, the lesser prairie-chicken (a type of bird with the scientific name Tympanuchus pallidicinctus) must be removed from the government's official lists of threatened and endangered species. This includes all different groups of lesser prairie-chickens wherever they live. These lists were created under a law called the Endangered Species Act of nineteen seventy-three. Part B changes that same law to prevent the lesser prairie-chicken from ever being put back on these protected species lists in the future. It adds new language saying that the Secretary (the government official in charge of this) cannot decide that the lesser prairie-chicken is threatened or endangered.
